message:
feat(resolv): implement __res_nameinquery / __res_queriesmatch predicates

Replace the prior stubs (which unconditionally returned 0) with a real
implementation of the resolver question-section predicates and their
internal `__libc_*` aliases. These functions are part of the public
libresolv ABI and are used by callers that need to introspect a DNS
message's question set without re-parsing it via the higher-level
`ns_initparse` machinery.

`__res_nameinquery(name, type, class, buf, eom)`:
  - Validates that `name` is a NUL-terminated C string and that
    `(buf, eom)` bound a readable region of at least the DNS header
    (`NS_HFIXEDSZ`) inside our tracked-region map.
  - Walks the QDCOUNT entries of the message starting at offset 12,
    using `dn_expand` to decompress each owner name and pulling the
    QTYPE/QCLASS pair out of the trailing fixed 4 bytes.
  - Returns 1 on the first question matching `(name, type, class)`
    (using `ns_samename` for case-insensitive label comparison),
    0 if no question matches, and -1 on malformed input.

`__res_queriesmatch(buf1, eom1, buf2, eom2)`:
  - Treats two DNS UPDATE-opcode messages (opcode 5 in the header
    flag word) as trivially "equivalent" before doing any question
    walking, mirroring glibc's libresolv shortcut.
  - Returns 0 immediately when QDCOUNTs differ, otherwise iterates
    msg1's questions and verifies each is also present in msg2 by
    delegating to `__res_nameinquery`.
  - Returns 1 on full match, 0 on mismatch, and -1 on malformed
    input.

The `__libc_res_nameinquery` / `__libc_res_queriesmatch` symbols in
`unistd_abi.rs` are converted from stubs into thin pass-through
aliases over the public versions, so the internal and public ABI
surfaces stay bit-for-bit identical and we don't drift two parallel
implementations.

Supporting helpers added to `resolv_abi.rs`:
  - `dns_message_slice` — bounds-checks `(buf, eom)`, enforces minimum
    DNS header length, and confirms the region is in the tracked
    pointer map before lifting it to a `&[u8]`.
  - `dns_header_opcode` — extracts the 4-bit opcode from the header
    flag word for the UPDATE-message shortcut.
  - `read_query_question` — single-question reader that returns
    `(next_pos, qtype, qclass)` and propagates `dn_expand` failures.
  - `NS_OPCODE_UPDATE` constant for clarity.

Tests added to `resolv_abi_test.rs`:
  - `res_query_nameinquery_finds_matching_question` — case-insensitive
    `FOO.com.` matches the encoded `foo.com` question, and a
    different QTYPE returns 0.
  - `res_query_queriesmatch_compares_question_sets` — identical
    queries return 1, different QTYPE returns 0.
  - `res_query_queriesmatch_accepts_update_opcode_headers` — two
    bare UPDATE headers compare as equivalent.
  - `res_query_predicates_reject_malformed_packets` — a header
    claiming QDCOUNT=1 but with no question bytes returns -1 from
    both predicates.
  - `res_query_internal_aliases_delegate_to_public_impl` — confirms
    `__libc_res_nameinquery` / `__libc_res_queriesmatch` produce the
    same answers as their public counterparts.

Replaces the obsolete `bd_dcfj5_res_nameinquery_and_queriesmatch_return_zero`
test which had been asserting the stub behavior.

Also includes a small rustfmt-only line wrap of `PUBLIC_ST_MASK` in
`unistd_abi.rs::statfs_to_statvfs` that landed alongside the resolver
edits in the same working tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
